intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct healthy specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but in a state of severe decline.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Pothos leaf present, but stem is failing)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No visible silver guest foliage)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; soil appears undisturbed but devoid of plant matter.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot contains only soil and debris; no mint growth detected.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf is present but showing signs of chlorosis and structural collapse; stem is thin and losing turgor.
plant_audit.P4: Merged with P2/P3 area; no distinct plant growth observed.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells in P3; electronic components (IC chips/pins) present on the desk surface as 'New Introductions'.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Minimal change; initial state shows P3 leaf with some turgor.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-2: Progressive loss of turgor in P3 leaf; stem appears to be thinning.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: P3 leaf has collapsed further; the stem is now nearly horizontal, indicating advanced senescence or root failure.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The Pothos (P3) is the only remaining specimen, and it is currently dying due to likely overwatering (indicated by dark, damp soil and lack of drainage) or root rot. The other registered plants are effectively lost.
anomalies: Presence of electronic components (IC chips) on the desk surface.
Crushed eggshells used as soil amendment/top dressing in P3.
White rabbit anchor is present but the surrounding plant life has vanished.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is failing. The Pothos in the black pot is the sole survivor, and it is in a state of terminal decline. The absence of the String of Nickels and Mexican Mint suggests a complete loss of those specimens. The presence of electronic debris suggests the desk is being used for non-botanical purposes, which may be contributing to the neglect of the plants.
